description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular body featuring a wood-textured top and sides, and a cream-colored front and back panel. The front face displays a large metallic speaker grille with a blue diamond-shaped emblem at the center, flanked by two prominent control knobs labeled for volume and tuning. Below the grille, a frequency indicator strip is visible, marked with AM, FM, and SW bands. The back panel is gray with ventilation slits, a caution label, a visible telescopic antenna, and power input ports. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining both metallic and plastic elements for durability and style. The object appears compact and portable, suitable for placement on tables or shelves in urban environments.
description: A compact, vintage-style radio receiver with a wood-textured body, cream front, metallic grille, and dual control knobs. Features include a frequency indicator, rear antenna, and power ports.
description_view_0: The front view shows the metallic speaker grille, blue diamond emblem, two large control knobs, and the frequency indicator strip.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wood-textured panel with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the gray back panel with ventilation slits, caution label, telescopic antenna, and power input ports.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood-textured panel without additional features.
